channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Crimson Desert Update Lets You Tame Special Mounts
item.content:encoded:
If you've been playing Pearl Abyss' tremendously popular Crimson Desert, you may have been wanting to change up your mount for something more exotic. Why travel around on a normal mount when far weirder options exist?

As part of Crimson Desert update 1.06 the following animals can be registered as 'special mounts':

  • Bears,
  • Boars,
  • Wolves,
  • Deer,
  • Mountain Goats,
  • Kuku Birds,
  • Iguanas,
  • Raptors,
  • Camels,
  • Lions, 
  • Tigers

Some of the animals will have unique taming methods, while those labeled ferocious will be tamable by subduing and then feeding them, gaining their trust. Feeding animals that aren't tamable won't impact their trust level. Saddles for special mounts can be found at different saddleries across the world, with 8 different saddlers having access to one or two special saddles each. Players will be able to equip both a special mount and a normal mount.

Beyond the special mounts and saddles, Crimson Desert update 1.06 also adds a new extraction feature. This feature can be used at various smithies and returns some of the material that was used to refine equipment, either for a single refinement level or all of them since acquired. Returns are based on material type - special materials like Artifacts and Aeserion's scale are recovered in full, while common materials like copper and iron return about 70% of the amount used.

Other changes include unarmed combat skills ('Fists') for Oongka, while Damiane will get some unarmed skills in the next update. Additionally, swords now have sheaths, which you can toggle an option on whether it is displayed or not. For those who want their canine friends to get in on the action, the new Sigil of Valor item, available from the Secret Shop NPC in Poroin, will have your pet dog attacks enemies when it is equipped. 

Beyond all of that there is a new claw machine at the Laughing Marionette with a number of items you can obtain from it. A number of smaller changes are also included in the update which you can read here.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don
